MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Dec-14-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Cochrane (Counsel for Eva Knott, Objector); Robert Mitchell (Counsel for Carol Mitchell, Petitioner).

As for the Motion for Summary Adjudication, counsel in general submitted the matter on the pleadings but Mr. Cochrane noted briefly in opposition that the issues presented by the Motion would not be dispositive of any issue or issues in the case but would instead function to shift the burden of proof at time of trial, not a proper function for a motion for summary adjudication. Mr. Mitchell noted that while burden shifting would be one consequence of the MSA, it could also be dispositive of certain affirmative defenses in the case. The Court took the matter under submission.

At the request of counsel, the matter was also referred to Dept. 1 for trial setting by Minute Order signed in open court.

B&P Code exempts members of the bar and their employees from the provision of 2020.420 which requires the deposition officer to be a registered photocopier.
